frameworks.name: policy compression
frameworks.one_line: resource-rational framework limiting complexity of state-action mappings under cognitive constraints

frameworks.name: action subsampling
frameworks.one_line: agents consider only a reduced subset of available actions to ease cognitive load

frameworks.name: resource-rational decision-making
frameworks.one_line: near-optimal behavior given fine-grained cognitive resource constraints

frameworks.name: contextual multi-armed bandit
frameworks.one_line: experimental paradigm validating predictions about action set and policy adaptation

core_question: How do constraints on action consideration sets and policy complexity jointly shape decision-making in large action spaces?
key_assumptions: cognitive resources for representing actions and policies are limited
agents can adapt both action set size and policy complexity to context
policy complexity and action subsampling interact in mitigating suboptimality
manipulations: task context in contextual multi-armed bandit
key_findings: reduced action consideration sets cause characteristic suboptimality patterns
policy complexity and action set size interact non-trivially in mitigating suboptimality
people preferentially sample generally valuable options when generating choices
responses across contexts become more correlated under cognitive load
humans flexibly adapt action sets and policy complexity to maintain near-optimality
interpretations: fine-grained resource constraints are essential for understanding human cognition
adaptive metacognitive strategies appear even in simple decision tasks
